reviews.review_text: Went from playing Plague inc on an IPad when I was younger to living it every 5 years or so in real life as an adult 10/10, Soundtrack is amazing too.
This is one of the first addicting game i've played in a while would genuinely play  for hours on end if health wasn't affected by gaming

This game is a brilliantly twisted strategy game that lets you play the ultimate villain, and looking back at how accurately it simulated global panic and government responses during real world events just makes it even more of a masterpiece. The core gameplay loop is incredibly addictive; you are constantly playing a tense game of chess against global health organizations, carefully managing your DNA points to mutate your pathogen. You have to balance infectivity, severity, and lethality perfectly mutate into a deadly strain too fast, and your hosts die before spreading it; stay hidden for too long, and you lose the momentum to infect everyone. Every veteran of this game knows the absolute, controller breaking frustration of seeing Greenland instantly close their seaports just because someone coughed on the other side of the planet. Beyond the standard viruses and bacteria, the game offers a massive amount of replayability with unique plagues like the necroa virus, mind controlling parasites, and even the cure expansion where the roles are reversed and you fight to save humanity. The UI is clean, the world map simulation is oddly mesmerizing to watch as everything turns red, and the steam workshop integration provides endless custom scenarios. It is a deceptively simple yet mechanically deep strategy simulator that turns the morbid concept of wiping out humanity into a highly replayable, tactical puzzle. A must have for strategy fans

Extremely repetitive, every run is basically the same and even the more unique scenarios are repetitive once you've already played them once. The newer content is not as good and now they're starting to paywall it.

I also take issue with requiring the game to communicate with Ndemic's servers in order to save any data. Not only does it make it pointless to play the game if you're offline, but if those servers are shut down then the save data is lost and progression stops working.

The game is good, but the main question I keep coming back to is why would you actually buy it on Steam?
Most people already know Plague Inc is a solid game, but the issue is that after one or two playthroughs with each sickness, you have probably already experienced around 80 percent of what the game has to offer. The remaining content mostly feels aimed at completionists or very dedicated players going for full 100 percent runs. 
Because of that, I find it hard to fully recommend this game unless it is on discount or you have never played it before. While it is definitely a well-made and interesting strategy game, the replay value for a typical player feels somewhat limited.

Fun little pandemic simulator. The only thing that might be off to some people is most of the available disease types do not really have any "active" abilities you can utilize (you just evolve traits and then watch it all play out), but there is enough side modes for you to enjoy (unique scenarios, more interesting or even unusual disease types, or the daily run mode with its own progression and randomized scenarios).

Much harder than i thought it would be. Been sat here for 3 hours trying to beat normal mode but maybe im just bad. Fun game to play when bored and the variety of diseases are great. I wouldnt say its worth the full price but its a great buy on sale.
Really good, easy to adapt to, overall great game

defintely NOT made for casual players, even at easy difficulty you need to play nice without any mistakes in order to win, and to progress through the game, you need to win aka kill ABSOLUTELY everyone, because killing 99% of humanity and leaving 1 human isnt enough for you to win. 

the game does not teaches you how to or when to upgrade something, or what countries are recommended to infect first, or not to infect. im not gonna go look through guides, just to understand how to play the game properly.

i only won twice, on normal difficulty and on tutorial, and never since.

even after trying to play aggressive, or laying low, you still wont have enough points to upgrade and rest of humanity will either survive or make a cure for it.

if looking into community made guides is essential, then that means the game is just mid.

EDIT: I learnt that you need to be lucky with events to win, and unmutate some mutations in time, if you're not lucky, then you need to load your game if you previously saved in time. 0/10 game.

Plague Inc: Evolved is a strategy simulation game developed by Ndemic Creations that offers an unconventional and highly engaging gameplay concept. Rather than focusing on crisis management, the game challenges players to design and evolve different types of pathogens while adapting to changing global conditions. This unique perspective immediately distinguishes it from traditional simulation titles and creates a surprisingly deep strategic experience.

The gameplay is built around resource management, long-term planning, and decision-making. At the beginning of each session, players select a pathogen type, each with different strengths and limitations that influence progression and strategy. As the simulation develops, players earn DNA points that can be invested into transmission methods, environmental adaptation, and symptom evolution. These systems interact closely, requiring players to balance expansion speed, visibility, and efficiency.

One of the game’s strongest features is its dynamic world simulation. Environmental conditions, transportation networks, regional characteristics, and adaptive responses continuously reshape the situation and force players to adjust their approach. The progression system rewards patience and strategic thinking rather than simple repetition. Even small decisions can significantly affect later stages of the game, making every playthrough feel different.

Visually, the PC version delivers a cleaner interface and smoother presentation that supports the complex systems without becoming overwhelming. The overall design remains accessible while maintaining meaningful depth for players who enjoy analytical gameplay.

I had been looking forward to playing Plague Inc: Evolved for a long time, and after finally spending a few hours with it, the experience exceeded my expectations. I was genuinely impressed by how detailed and immersive the gameplay felt. The combination of strategic depth, unpredictable outcomes, and constant decision-making made it extremely enjoyable, and it quickly became one of the most memorable strategy games I have played recently.

I guess it can be recommended, but in full honesty, there are way better games to invest your time and money.
Plague Inc was first introduced to me during global pandemic (WHAT A SURPRISE, RIGHT?), I tried it, managed to beat several levels and got bored.
Now I saw it on a huge discount (70% if I am not wrong), bought it again, played for a bit, got bored again and I don't think I will be back soon.
The game is just too repetitive and too RNG dependent. Even if your strategy is perfect, there is a risk whole run will be lost due to bad luck.

I recommend this game only on a big discount, for players who didn't have a chance to play it during lockdown. You will enjoy it, for a couple of hours.

A solid simulator that can easily keep you entertained for hours while showing just how dangerous both human-made and natural threats can be. The game does a great job of demonstrating the impact of diseases and why hygiene and prevention are so important.

That said, mastering the game takes some effort. Unlocking every disease isn't easy, and you'll need to spend time learning the mechanics and experimenting with different strategies. If you enjoy a challenge and like simulation games, this one is definitely worth trying.

I love this game so much. It's so addictive! I love how you can strategize and think of different scenarios in order to mix and match it. I also like how it makes you think on the go.

However, I hoped there was RNG scenarios or buffs as it can get slightly repetitive at a certain point. So much fun playing alone or with a partner! It's also a great game to play with a companion as it actually requires lots of collaboration

Plague inc: evolved is a great game!
The objective is simple, and you can select difficulties and further find community-made or official scenarios!
Overall I would recommend if you are good at strategy.

fun game. Once you get the hang of popping bubbles and when and what to upgrade, can be a great time killer...and obviously learn to evolve...to do better
The medical details, roleplaying, and fiction are very very good. But unfortunately some countries are missing or inaccurate.

Too frustrating and heavily reliant on luck
I really wanted to like this game, but the difficulty curve is absolutely absurd. Even on the normal settings, it feels almost impossible to win without following a highly specific, step-by-step guide from the internet.
The main issue is the AI magic comeback. You can spend 20 minutes perfectly crafting a stealthy, non-lethal virus to infect the entire world. But the second you evolve a single symptom or someone sneezes, the entire globe instantly locks down their borders, closes seaports, and pumps out a cure in what feels like two minutes.
It is incredibly frustrating to lose right at the finish line because Greenland or Madagascar decided to completely isolate themselves before your disease could land. The gameplay relies way too much on random mutations and luck rather than actual strategy. It stops being fun and just becomes a chore of restarting over and over again. Save your money unless you enjoy stressful trial-and-error simulators.
Some much fun, just like back in school, but better.

Just as good gameplay as the original web browser game but consistently updated and smooth as silk. The achievements, free Neurax Worm updates and Outbreak mode included in the base version make it super worth it even without the DLCs. It can be a tricky game to figure out, but once you hit the loop of infectivity growth, moderate severity and then figuring out just the right time to go lethal when evolution is slowing down, that makes studying the scenarios really satisfying. Anyway 10/10, would infect Madagascar again.

That + some clickable bubbles to make you think its not just a text-order game with "graphics".

Plague Inc: Evolved is a unique game where the goal is to bring humanity to extinction with the viruses that you make. I do want to say that the game is fun with it's game mechanics like upgrading your virus so that has better ways to transmit the disease, placing the origin of the virus in populated areas and finding better ways to infect the planet.

The reasons that I personally don't like the game is that it feels slow and repetitive. Every time I started a new virus it felt the same way of placing it down and waiting for it to spread and using the same upgrade to kill humanity. Their were some daily events that changed how I approached the game, but in the end I still chose the same upgrades which still felt repetitive and annoying to go through again.

Maybe I haven't played the game enough, but I still feel like it would be lost in my game library after 3 hours of gameplay...
Cool game, diverse plagues. This infection RNG pisses me off though, many times I just have to restart because the game refuses to send an infected ship to some island.

Very fun overall. Lots of plague types, scenarios, and things to do in general. My one critique is that once you do everything the game starts feeling a bit bland. I'd love to see more additions to the scenario creator because it feels a bit limited right now. Players in other games like Geometry Dash and Mario Maker have the ability to make insanely creative levels with the in-game editors that are provided, and I feel like Plague Inc. could have a similar thing, but it's just not there right now. Much love to the developers though as they do incredible work!

The game is amazing but lacks some relatability, though custom scenarios can more than make up for that. I would like to see cure mode expansions in the future as the foundation is great and I feel there could be some really good implementations that could make the mode even better but overall it’s great!

Ontop of this, none of the mechanics besides the very basic ones are explained.
So if you want a bio weapons but are stuck on the zombie virus then well... ♥♥♥♥ you LMAO.

The only and the same world (usually, there are some modificators for specific viruses etc) against a ton of diseases or invasions. You're the bad guy here, so do your worst. Still need to think one step ahead, though: just making a deadly virus is not enough. Good luck. Put the world into ashes and chaos.

The game succeeds as a strategic sandbox built around experimentation, adaptation, and timing. The main scenarios are enjoyable, and the custom scenarios add plenty of replay value and variety. Gameplay is simple to understand but still engaging enough to keep you experimenting with different approaches. It may not go far beyond its formula, but what it offers, it executes well.

- There should be 0 reason why I'm addicted to this game, but here we are.
If you're completely new to Plague Inc, it's a hyper realistic infection disaster game.
You have a goal to infect the whole world, stay hidden or fight the cure, and at the end wipe the whole humanity down to absolute zero!

- Veteran Core
Be aware, many of us rage because Madagascar & Greenland refuses to surrender that easy to you, so be ready to punch them first before they close their ship ports and make you lose the game.

- Content
Even if you finish all bacteria you can try custom scenarios made by developers themselves, trust me the modifiers are hella fun to play around.

- Would i recommend Plague Inc?
Absolutely, it's a unique game to play, it can hook you for hours on end until you fully learn how to unlock many other types of diseases, each of 'em have unique playstyles, so you'll have time and fun learning them.

- Multiplayer Aspect
Forgot to mention, you can grab a buddy to play together in Co-Op or ruin your friendship by 1v1'ing to see who can be more deadly, Co-Op features teamwork aspect as you both have to stay silent or else development will try to shut you down, you can help your teammate by mutating your virus to assist, you both have to destroy the world.

So what are you waiting for, go destroy humanity in most unique ways possible!!
its kinda annoying asf i hate playing as the fungus but other than that its good the custom scenario is fun

My only complaint about this game is trying to achieve a victory with the necroa virus it is VERY annoying and Idk if I'm just bad at the game but it feels like if you mess up even once your run can get halted permanently and cause you to restart the run. Besides that I think it's a game that has definitely stood up against time over the past 10 years.
